segments.text: [MUSIC PLAYING]

TAYLOR LEHMAN:
Welcome to I/O 2025.

I'm Taylor, a product
lead for Google Home.

And I'm absolutely thrilled to
be back at I/O with all of you.

Last year at I/O, we
announced the Home APIs,

enabling developers to
build apps and experiences
for the home.

The Device and
Structure APIs enable

developers to control
and manage devices
connected to Google Home.

And there are now over
750 million devices
accessible via the Home APIs.

The Commissioning
API makes it easier

for users to set up and share
devices with other apps using
Google's Matter infrastructure.

The automations API
gives developers

access to Google's
intelligent automation engine.

And the Home APIs were
designed from the ground up

to be cross-platform,
supporting both Android and iOS.

Today, we'll look at what
developers have already

built on the Home APIs
and talk about what you
can expect in the year ahead.

And it shouldn't be
any surprise at all

that Gemini plays a big
role in what comes next.

Last year, we began the
journey of integrating
Gemini with Google Home.

We've created new Google
Home app features,

such as enabling users to search
their video history and see

event descriptions or one of
my favorites, "help me create"

that lets you make intelligent
automations using natural
language.

We've also enabled users
to control their smart home

devices using natural language
directly in the Gemini app.

And later this year, we're
bringing these same features

to the Home APIs, so you can
offer the power of Gemini
in your app as well.

But before we dive deeper into
how Gemini is revolutionizing

the home, I want
to take a moment

to celebrate the amazing
experiences our partners have

already built. Last
year at I/O, we

highlighted how ADT, LG,
Eve, and others were already

using the Home APIs in
their own experiences.

The first of these apps are
already live in the Play Store.
You can check them out today.

Since then, even more
partners are actively

developing innovative
experiences
powered by the Home APIs.

Let's take a look at a
few of those together.

The Cync app uses
the Home APIs to make

sure your pet remains
comfortable, even
if you are at work for the day.

With access to Google's
devices and intelligence,

Cync can make sure the
lighting and temperature are

just right from the time
you leave until when you
return at the end of your day.

The new iRobot app,
powered by the Home APIs,

combines iRobot's
Matter-enabled vacuum

with Google's intelligence to
solve a problem we all hate,

having to clean up the
place we call home.

With the Home APIs,
the iRobot vacuum

will automatically
start its cleaning cycle
as you leave for work.

And thanks to a
seamless integration

with your smart
home's away signals,

that's one less
thing to think about.

Motorola is exploring how
the Moto Tag could instantly

trigger a custom
smart home routine,

like turning off
the coffee machine,

adjusting the thermostat, or
starting your favorite playlist.

The access to a wide
range of devices

and Google's automation
infrastructure provided

by the Home APIs makes
these experiences possible.

And Tuya Smart is
making the smart home
more seamless than ever.

Because the apps
use the Home APIs,

Matter devices set up
in the Tuya Smart app

will automatically be available
in the Google Home app.

And all the lights the user
has connected to Google Home

can be controlled in
the Tuya Smart app

without setting them
up a second time.

Because of Tuya
Smart's innovation,

their users can spend less
time managing their smart home
and more time enjoying it.

These are just a few of
the examples of what's
possible with the Home APIs.

You can look for these apps and
others coming to the Play Store

and App Store in
the coming months.

Now, let's shift our attention
to the main topic for today,

bringing Gemini-powered
features to the Home APIs.

There are three things I
want to talk about today.

First, we're bringing cameras to
the Home APIs powered by Gemini.

Second, we are introducing
new Gemini-powered features

to make automations
easier and more natural.

And third, I'll share how your
devices get the power of Gemini

when you integrate
with Google Home.

With the Home APIs,
our goal is to enable

all developers to build
innovative experiences
for the home.

And as you've seen from the
examples I shared earlier,

developers are already
doing incredible things.

Now, with Gemini
and the Home APIs,

we're taking the next step,
bringing the best of Google's AI
directly to you.

We're moving beyond
simple device control

to create an effortless
smart home that

truly understands, adapts, and
responds to your users' needs.

The next era of the smart
home powered by Gemini

isn't just about
connected devices.

It's about creating
effortless experiences.

Google Home users love
to use their Nest cameras

to receive intelligent
notifications,

view events from their
camera's history,

and use two-way talk to greet
people at the front door.

Last fall, we added new
Gemini-powered camera features

into public preview in
the Google Home app,

allowing users to
ask questions like,

did the kids leave their
bikes in the driveway?

And see a list of
relevant clips.

And developers understand
how important cameras
are to the home.

You've made that loud and clear
in all of our conversations.

So that's why I'm so
excited to announce

that we're bringing all of
the Nest camera experiences
to the Home APIs.

This includes all the
standard camera features

you'd expect, livestream,
history, two-way talk, camera

settings, and of course, all of
the Gemini-powered intelligence
features that users love.

With intelligent
notifications, your users

can receive notifications
from your app

when a familiar
face has been seen
or a package has been delivered.

And with Gemini-powered event
descriptions and camera history

search, your users can
use natural language

to search their video
history directly in your app.

One of the themes I've heard
from talking to developers

is that their users
love the value they

get from intelligent
automations,

but they don't know
where to get started.

We want to help you
solve this problem

with the new
Gemini-powered features

we're bringing to
the automations API.

With suggested
automations, Gemini

looks at the devices
in a user's home

and suggests automations
that may be useful for them.

"Help me create" makes building
an automation as easy as using

everyday language to tell
Gemini what you want to do.

And we're also
adding new starters

based on date and weather,
so your automations

can better respond to
the complexities of life.

These features mean
less development time

for you and more intelligent,
personalized experiences
for your users.

In addition to building
Gemini-powered experiences

in your own app, when you
integrate with Google Home,

your devices can take part
in Gemini-powered experiences
across all of Google's surfaces.

For example, Google Home
can connect with Gemini

so your users can
control and ask

about devices directly
from their Gemini
app using natural language.

And we've previewed
Gemini on home devices

like smart speakers, smart
displays, and Google TV.

With Gemini capabilities, users
get more natural and helpful

voice experiences with
better understanding

that lets users easily
explore any topic,

control smart home devices,
and even create automations
just using their voice.

We're also testing
a home status widget
with a select group of users.

It's powered by Gemini
Nano on Pixel devices

and gives users a
personalized, real time summary

of their smart home based on
their usage and preferences
right on their home screen.

This means instant access to
the most important information

about your smart devices
without even opening an app.

Starting later this year, we'll
open the Gemini-powered Home

APIs to a small group
of early access partners
to begin testing.

The best way to get ready
for a Gemini-powered future

is to start building
your Home APIs app today.

To help inspire your
creativity, we're

launching a series of
themed developer challenges,

giving you a chance
to show off demos

of what you've built
with the Home APIs
and win some fantastic prizes.

Get all the details by signing
up for our smart home developer

newsletter at the
link on your screens.

And be among the first to access
the new home API capabilities

and receive updates about our
upcoming developer challenges.

The Home APIs are your gateway
to an incredible future.

Dive in today, start
building, and get
ready to reimagine the home.
[MUSIC PLAYING]
